subject: DevOps
title: Virtual Private Clouds (VPC)
description: A quick, practical guide to Virtual Private Clouds (VPC).

hook.text: Millions share the public cloud. How do you isolate your network? A VPC carves out private space.

buildup.text: A VPC is a logically isolated network. You define IP ranges, subnets, route tables, and gateways.

discovery.text: Public subnets reach the internet. Private ones don't. DBs go private, load balancers go public.

twist.text: VPC peering and transit gateways link VPCs securely without routing through the public internet.

climax.text: Security groups and network ACLs act as firewalls controlling traffic in and out of resources.

punchline.text: A VPC gives you a private network in the public cloud.

quiz.question: Why should databases typically be placed in private subnets?
options: To prevent direct internet access and reduce the attack surface
Because databases don't need any network connectivity
Private subnets have faster network speeds
